sanskrit: वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते। गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते।।
transliteration: vepathuś ca śarīre me roma-harṣaś ca jāyate gāṇḍīvaṃ sraṃsate hastāt tvak caiva paridahyate
literal_meaning: Trembling of the body, horripilation, the bow Gāṇḍīva slipping from the hand, and the skin burning.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s nervous system is in full fight-or-flight: body shaking, hair erect, weapon falling, skin on fire. These visceral signs mark the moment when abstract moral dread collapses into raw somatic panic, revealing how existential conflict can hijack the body before the mind can even name the fear.
psychological_state: A warrior-hero whose body is betraying him as the reality of imminent violence shatters his self-concept.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon about to operate on a loved one feels her hands shake and instruments slip the instant she recognizes the patient on the table.
anchor_text: When dread grips the body, stillness is the first battlefield.
